definitions: Hermann von Helmholtz (1821‚Äì1894), a German physician and physicist who made significant contributions to several scientific fields, including the physiology of vision and hearing, electrodynamics, and thermodynamics.
A crater on the Moon, named after Hermann von Helmholtz.
A crater on Mars, named after Hermann von Helmholtz.
examples: Helmholtz's work on the conservation of energy was groundbreaking.
The Helmholtz equation is fundamental in physics.
We observed the Helmholtz crater on the lunar surface.
